hard.next_step: Marc to share the Zycus deck and modular AI capability overview with Justin Chang. Justin Chang to review internally with the portfolio procurement leadership team post-Memorial Day. Schedule a follow-up discussion in 3-4 weeks focused on intake orchestration, sourcing automation, and Oracle/SAP integration feasibility across portfolio companies. Request 1-2 operational inputs from Roark Capital (e.g., sourcing volume, ERP landscape, auto-renewal trends) to tailor an ROI-driven demo and discussion.
hard.ais_why: Justin Chang showed interest in Zycus' agentic AI approach, particularly beyond traditional LLM-based procurement automation. The team was engaged by the intake-to-outcome workflow vision, including autonomous sourcing and negotiation capabilities. Interest increased around modular AI agents that can integrate with existing Oracle, SAP, and Coupa environments without requiring a full platform replacement.
hard.competitor: Oracle (incumbent ERP at Roark); status quo / in-house spend tools; Coupa referenced as an integration environment

north_star_verdict.headline: Rich 20 May intro call with Justin Chang, but the buyer has gone quiet through two unanswered follow-ups (1 Jun, 11 Jun) and the post-Memorial-Day review he promised never landed. Advisory-only contact, no spend authority, single-thread. Close date May 2027 is far off, so the issue is thinning engagement, not the date.
north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 27 May 2026, Qualified stage, 313 days to close (15 May 2027). Last real two-way buyer exchange was the 20 May intro call, ~47 days ago; last logged activity 11 Jun was a rep-side email (no buyer reply). Two rep follow-ups (1 Jun, 11 Jun) went unanswered, so the 3-4 week cadence Justin set has slipped. Not yet cold (under 60 days of buyer silence), but a key thread is going quiet.
evidence: Introductory discovery call completed 20 May 2026 with Justin Chang (Head of Supply Chain, Roark Capital portfolio operations); he sought Zycus out after the ISM Merlin demo
Justin stated he controls no direct spend or vendor decisions: 'I don't control any direct spend decision, vendor decisions, across our portfolio' (Avoma, 20 May)
Two rep follow-up emails on 1 Jun and 11 Jun (Clari) drew no buyer reply; the promised post-Memorial-Day internal review has not surfaced
Buyer signalled low urgency: 'there's no flashing red light right now' (Avoma, 20 May) - a strategic/exploratory evaluation, not a burning buy
No economic buyer, no decision maker, and no brand-level CPO mapped; the deal is single-threaded through an advisory contact

metrics.narrative: Justin Chang articulated clear operational pain on the 20 May call but quantified none of it. He described lean procurement teams across portfolio brands that leave a 'long tail' of categories where they 'just don't have the bandwidth' so they 'auto renew' or run a 'very not robust sourcing event', plus rogue/out-of-contract spend and uneven source-to-contract maturity. He also flagged messy spend cubes that his team cleans with in-house machine-learning tools. No savings target, cycle-time, headcount, or ROI figure was named, and Justin said plainly 'there's no flashing red light right now'. Quantifying the value is the move: the documented next step already asks Roark for sourcing volume, ERP landscape, and auto-renewal trends to model an ROI-driven case.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang
SF Next_Step__c (request operational inputs to tailor an ROI-driven demo)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped, and Justin Chang explicitly disqualified himself as one: 'I will either work on very specific value creation projects... I don't control any direct spend decision, vendor decisions, across our portfolio' and 'I'd have to ask our CFO and or CIO' (Avoma, 20 May). Roark is a centralized PE investor with decentralized systems - Justin was clear the brand-level chief supply chain officers and CPOs own direct spend and vendor selection, and that Roark 'doesn't sign any contracts on behalf' of portfolio companies. So budget authority sits with a brand-level CFO/CPO who has not been named in Salesforce or on any call. Identifying and reaching that budget owner through Justin is a ranked move; naming one now would be a guess.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang
SF OpportunityContactRole (only activity contact is Justin Chang)

decision_criteria.narrative: Justin named the criteria that matter to him even though no formal evaluation is underway. On 20 May he pressed on agentic differentiation ('what are the differentiators between the players out there... the folks that are AI first, AI led'), modular pick-and-choose deployment ('each wedge of that... Merlin wheel, you can pick and choose'), and ERP integration feasibility ('what does that integration look like... you'll integrate with any major ERP'). The discrete agents he cared about were intake orchestration, autonomous sourcing/negotiation (Anna), and contract analysis for savings. These are inferred evaluation criteria from a single discovery call, not a documented BRD or scorecard, so a formal criteria set still needs to be confirmed with the brand procurement leaders.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang
SF AIS_Why__c (agentic AI, autonomous sourcing/negotiation, Oracle/SAP/Coupa integration)

decision_process.narrative: The process is genuinely unclear because of Roark's operating model. Justin framed himself as a central advisory/functional lead who surfaces themes to brand CPOs rather than a buyer: 'there's a broader... what speaking role do we as Roark want to have with our CPOs on this' and 'you start getting into enterprise tech conversations' (Avoma, 20 May). So the real question is whether Roark drives a centralized recommendation or each brand evaluates independently, and Justin himself said he needs to talk internally before deciding whether a demo makes sense. No approval chain, procurement channel, or timeline is documented. Mapping how Roark and its brands actually decide is a required next step, ideally in the follow-up Justin promised.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang
SF Next_Step__c (Justin to review internally with portfolio procurement leadership)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced - expected for a Qualified, 30-day-old net-new deal with no commercial conversation yet. Justin made one structurally important point: Roark 'doesn't sign any contracts on behalf' of its portfolio companies and needs everything 'super portable' in case a company is sold, so any eventual paper would run through a brand entity, not Roark centrally. Products in scope are iContract, iSource, and Merlin Intake (a multi-module S2C+intake footprint, so a full MSA plus Order Form and SOW motion if it advances). Visibility will come only once a specific pilot brand and its procurement/legal owner are identified.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated, if not urgent. Justin owns supply chain and procurement across Roark's franchise-model portfolio and described two consistent problems on 20 May: lean procurement orgs that cannot cover a 'long tail' of categories (leading to auto-renewals and weak sourcing events on maverick spend), and uneven source-to-contract/procure-to-pay governance producing 'rogue spend, out of contract spend'. The pain owner is Justin and, ultimately, the brand-level procurement teams he supports as 'extra arms and legs'. It is confirmed but qualitative and low-urgency - he explicitly said 'there's no flashing red light right now', which makes do-nothing the main threat rather than a competitor.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang

champion.narrative: Justin Chang (Head of Supply Chain, Roark Capital portfolio operations) is a developing but at-risk champion. He initiated the relationship after the ISM iCentro Merlin demo, took the full suite walkthrough, and asked to receive the deck to 'sit with my team and talk through it' (Avoma, 20 May) - genuine advocacy behaviour. What is developed: real AI curiosity and a willingness to socialize Zycus internally. What is missing and why the rating is not stronger: he has no budget or vendor authority, he is a single thread, and he has not delivered the promised post-Memorial-Day review or responded to the 1 Jun and 11 Jun follow-ups, so his engagement is cooling. His advocacy has to convert into a brand CPO/CFO introduction to matter.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang
SF activities 1 Jun and 11 Jun 2026 (unanswered rep follow-ups)

competition.narrative: There is no active competitive bake-off; the dominant alternative is the status quo. Justin said Roark's brands run 'a variety of tools' and have 'built our own modules internally' for spend analytics, and he was unsure of the ERP even at Roark ('Probably Oracle. I don't fully know', 20 May). Marc (Zycus) named Coupa only as an integration environment the intake agent can sit on, not as a rival Justin is evaluating, and Justin raised no competitor by name. His ISM goal was to understand 'the differentiators between the players out there', so a formal multi-vendor evaluation is possible but undisclosed. The real threat is inertia - lean teams that auto-renew and in-house tooling - so the wedge is homegrown agentic automation (Merlin Intake, Anna, AI risk radar) that acts autonomously versus LLM-only copilots, delivered modularly on top of the existing Oracle/SAP estate.
sources: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang

deal_movement.summary: Early-cycle deal that opened well then went quiet. The 20 May intro call was strong and the deck was shared, but the buyer has not responded to two rep follow-ups (1 Jun, 11 Jun) and the promised post-Memorial-Day internal review has not surfaced. The only recent change is an internal division re-tag on 3 Jul, not a buyer signal.

items.change: Introductory discovery call held with Justin Chang; full S2P suite walkthrough delivered; deck offered
Stage moved Initial Interest -> Qualified; typed New Business; owner set to Marc Quessenberry
Rep follow-up email sent asking for team feedback and a deep-dive discovery call (no buyer reply)
Second rep follow-up email referencing the prior conversation (no buyer reply)
Internal Division re-tagged Zycus -> New Markets (hygiene, not a buyer signal)

competitive_position.summary: There is no active competitor being evaluated against Zycus as of the 20 May 2026 call; the single strongest current threat is the status quo / do-nothing - lean brand teams that auto-renew long-tail categories and in-house spend tools Justin's team built, against 'no flashing red light' urgency. Oracle is the probable incumbent ERP at Roark (Justin unsure) and represents integration inertia rather than a procurement rival. Coupa was named by Zycus only as an integration environment, not a buyer-evaluated rival. Justin's ISM goal to compare AI-led 'players out there' implies a future shortlist that has not been disclosed.

competitors.name: Status quo / in-house spend tools (do-nothing)
competitors.quote: There's no flashing red light right now... we've built our own modules internally... leveraging all the spend data that we do get from our portfolio companies.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang
competitors.how_we_win: Make the cost of inertia concrete: quantify auto-renew and maverick-spend leakage on one pilot brand, then show Anna autonomous negotiation and Merlin Intake capturing savings the lean team cannot reach today.

competitors.name: Oracle (probable incumbent ERP at Roark / portfolio)
competitors.quote: Probably Oracle. I'll be honest with you, I don't think I fully know.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang
competitors.how_we_win: Oracle is ERP, not agentic S2P. Position Zycus as the AI layer on top via prebuilt Oracle hooks (no rip-and-replace), delivering intake orchestration and autonomous sourcing Oracle cannot.

competitors.name: Coupa (integration environment, not a stated rival)
competitors.quote: Our intake agent can reside on another platform if you had someone say out of Coupa... our intake agent or our Anna agent can also sit on other platforms.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026 (Marc, Zycus - named as an integration example; Justin did not object or confirm use)
competitors.how_we_win: If a brand runs Coupa, offer modular coexistence: put Merlin Intake and Anna on top of Coupa to deliver agentic sourcing Coupa's LLM-only approach lacks.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits Justin's stated wants on technology (agentic AI, modular deployment, ERP integration) but is exposed on the buying model: Justin is an advisory portfolio lead who owns no direct spend, so expectations about who buys and how remain unaligned.

best_practice_check.summary: Early net-new deal with genuine AI pull but no power access and cooling engagement. The win-strategy priorities are to multi-thread past an advisory contact, convert curiosity into a scoped pilot, and restart a stalled cadence.
flags: Multi-thread past Justin: he owns no direct spend, so reach a brand-level CPO/CFO who does before the deal can advance.
Convert exploratory interest into a concrete pilot - name 1-2 portfolio brands and anchor an ROI-driven demo to a real spend base.
Re-establish cadence: the buyer has been silent since 20 May through two follow-ups, so a value-led touch is needed to restart momentum.

items.name: Justin Chang
items.title: Head of Supply Chain, Roark Capital Portfolio Operations Group
items.sentiment: Positive but cooling - engaged and curious on 20 May, initiated contact after ISM, but unresponsive to 1 Jun and 11 Jun follow-ups.
items.risk: Advisory role with no direct spend or vendor authority and single-threaded; must introduce a brand CPO/CFO or the deal stalls. Overdue on the promised internal review.
items.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026; SF activity contact

champion_strength.summary: Justin Chang is a developing, at-risk champion. He drove the initial contact after the ISM Merlin demo, took the full suite walkthrough, and asked to socialize the deck internally - real advocacy. But he has no budget or vendor authority, is a single thread, and has not delivered the promised post-Memorial-Day review or answered two follow-ups (1 Jun, 11 Jun), so his engagement is cooling. He becomes a real champion only if he introduces a brand-level CPO/CFO.
champion_strength.champion: Justin Chang (Head of Supply Chain, Roark Capital Portfolio Operations)
champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026; SF Next_Step__c; SF activities 1 Jun and 11 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Agentic AI positioning landed well: Marc walked Justin through Merlin Intake (Teams-embedded control tower), Anna autonomous negotiation, and AI risk radar, and Justin engaged specifically on agents that 'perform a function' beyond LLMs. The story is being carried by the deal owner; the risk is it stays a generic suite walkthrough rather than a scoped, ROI-anchored demo against a real Roark spend base.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate reads accurately against the call. Justin actively sought out the agentic AI story after the ISM Merlin demo and pressed on differentiation versus LLM-only tools and Oracle/SAP integration, but framed it as exploratory ('somewhere down the road, could this be a fit') with no urgency. Curious, not hungry.

items.detail: No economic buyer or decision maker mapped; the only contact is an advisory portfolio lead who owns no direct spend and cannot sign.
items.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang

items.detail: Do-nothing risk: 'no flashing red light', lean teams auto-renew, and Roark builds in-house tools - inertia, not a rival, is the main threat.
items.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang

items.detail: Single-threaded through Justin with no access to the brand-level CPOs/CFOs who own vendor and budget decisions across the franchise portfolio.
items.source: Avoma intro call 20 May 2026, Justin Chang

items.detail: Oracle/SAP integration feasibility across diverse, decentralized portfolio systems is asserted (prebuilt hooks, 1,121 APIs) but unproven for any specific brand.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling. The 20 May call was strong, but the buyer has not replied to two rep follow-ups (1 Jun, 11 Jun) and the promised post-Memorial-Day review has not appeared. Under 60 days of silence, so recoverable, but the cadence Justin set has slipped.
items: Buyer silent since the 20 May intro call; two rep follow-ups unanswered (1 Jun, 11 Jun)
Promised post-Memorial-Day internal review not surfaced as of 6 Jul
Single thread through an advisory contact with no power access

critical_signals.text: The 20 May introductory call went well: Justin Chang engaged on agentic AI, asked for the deck, and probed Oracle/SAP integration, but he has since gone quiet.
No rival is being evaluated; the real threat is inertia, with lean brand teams auto-renewing long-tail spend and Justin saying there is no flashing red light.
Only Justin is engaged, and he owns no direct spend; the brand CPOs and CFO who actually buy have never been met.
No budget owner is identified and no pricing conversation has started; Justin said he would have to ask the CFO or CIO.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A promising 20 May intro call with an AI-curious advisory lead, Justin Chang, has stalled: he owns no spend authority, has gone silent through two follow-ups, and the deal's real opponent is Roark simply doing nothing.

win_position.text: This is an early, exploratory portfolio-operations conversation - Justin Chang engaged genuinely on agentic AI on 20 May but is advisory-only and cannot buy for any brand.
No economic buyer is mapped: Justin said he does not control any direct spend or vendor decisions and would have to ask the CFO or CIO (Avoma, 20 May).
The buyer has gone quiet since 20 May through two unanswered rep follow-ups (1 Jun, 11 Jun), and the post-Memorial-Day review he promised never landed.
Real AI pull is present: Justin sought Zycus out after the ISM Merlin demo and pressed on agentic differentiation and Oracle/SAP integration (Avoma, 20 May).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is thin - no live demo booked, no concrete buyer date, and the last genuine two-way exchange was the 20 May intro call.
Two rep follow-ups on 1 Jun and 11 Jun drew no buyer reply, so the 3-4 week cadence Justin set has already slipped.

customer_commitment.text: Justin committed only to review the deck with his team and consider a demo - no Zycus-specific step like a CPO intro or a proposal request (Avoma, 20 May).
He proactively initiated contact after ISM and took the full suite walkthrough, showing genuine curiosity about intake, Anna, and contract analysis (Avoma, 20 May).

deal_risk.text: The primary risk is inertia: Justin said 'there's no flashing red light right now' and Roark builds its own in-house spend tools - a no-decision risk, not a loss to a rival.
The deal is single-threaded through an advisory contact with no access to the brand CPO/CFO who owns direct spend and vendor selection.

deal_momentum.evidence: Buyer silent since 20 May; two rep follow-ups (1 Jun, 11 Jun) unanswered; no demo or buyer-named date.

customer_commitment.evidence: Only agreed to review the deck internally; advisory contact with no spend authority; no Zycus-specific next step.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing is the main threat ('no flashing red light'); single-thread with no economic buyer mapped.

customer_preference.evidence: Justin was curious but non-committal: 'I'll sit with my team and talk through it... whether it makes sense to do a demo.' No Zycus-specific ask (no CPO intro, no proposal) and he has not responded since 20 May.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only - lean teams, auto-renewals, maverick and out-of-contract spend - with no savings, cycle-time, or ROI figure, and Justin's own 'no flashing red light right now'.
